15.reason: [The loop breaks when m1[i] == 0, but the problem does not guarantee that the constant term (exponent 0) is the last input or even present. This causes premature termination if a term with exponent 0 appears before the end of input. Also, input reading should stop at newline, not based on exponent value.]

21.reason: [Same error as above: assuming exponent 0 marks end of input, which is incorrect per problem statement. Input ends with newline, and terms can be in any order (though typically descending), but exponent 0 may not be last or may not exist.]
